title: Shenzhen XX Tech Co., failed to obtain CCC for smart cameras, fined RMB 50,000
summary: In March 2025, Shenzhen XX Tech declared export of "smart network cameras" under HS Code 8525.89. Customs inspection found the products were subject to CCC requirements but bore no CCC mark. The company was fined RMB 50,000 and required to re-export the goods.

title: Guangzhou XX fined for unlicensed export of encrypted routers
summary: Guangzhou XX declared routers with embedded encryption chips for export. As encryption products subject to the Dual-Use Items Control List, they required a license that was not obtained. The company was fined and the export was stopped.

title: Shanghai Customs seized undeclared lithium battery shipments
summary: Shanghai Customs inspection found multiple shipments of wireless earbuds with built-in lithium batteries that lacked proper dangerous goods transport documentation. Shipper was fined and goods were held until documentation was rectified.

title: Drone exporter fined for lacking Dual-Use export license
summary: A Shenzhen-based drone exporter declared commercial drones under HS code 8525.89 without obtaining the required Dual-Use Items export license. Customs inspection flagged the goods as controlled items. The exporter was fined and prohibited from exporting for 3 months.

title: Power banks seized at Shanghai port for missing CCC certification
summary: Shanghai Customs seized a shipment of 2,000 power banks from a Shenzhen exporter. The power banks lacked CCC certification marks. The exporter was fined RMB 30,000 and the goods were ordered to be re-exported after certification was obtained.

title: Smart watches without SRRC approval blocked at Shenzhen Customs
summary: A batch of Bluetooth-enabled smart watches was stopped at Shenzhen Customs. The exporter had obtained CCC certification but failed to secure SRRC Type Approval for the Bluetooth radio module. Goods were held until SRRC documentation was submitted.

title: Smart home hub devices fined for missing SRRC and encryption review
summary: A consignment of smart home hubs with Wi-Fi and Zigbee radios was flagged by Ningbo Customs. The products lacked SRRC approval and had not undergone commercial encryption review. Total fines exceeded RMB 80,000.

title: Unregistered medical devices rejected at Beijing Customs
summary: Beijing Customs rejected a shipment of home-use blood pressure monitors and pulse oximeters because the exporter could not provide NMPA medical device registration certificates. The goods were ordered to be returned at exporter's cost.

title: Fast chargers with false energy labels detained by Chinese customs
summary: A batch of GaN fast chargers imported into China was detained because the energy efficiency labels were either missing or did not match the registered values. The importer was required to correct labeling and pay a fine of RMB 20,000.

title: Car DVR dash cams blocked for missing CCC and wireless approval
summary: Guangzhou Customs blocked a shipment of Wi-Fi-enabled car dash cameras. The exporter had assumed the products were exempt, but they fell under CCC (IT equipment) and required SRRC for the Wi-Fi module. The exporter was fined and required to complete both certifications.

title: Bluetooth earphones imported without SRRC approval fined at Shanghai Port
summary: Shanghai Customs detained 3,000 units of TWS Bluetooth earphones imported from Vietnam. The importer had CCC certification but was unaware that the Bluetooth function required separate SRRC radio type approval before importation. A fine of RMB 38,000 was imposed and the goods were held until SRRC documentation was provided.

title: LED projectors with false CCC marks seized by Ningbo Customs
summary: Ningbo Customs seized a container of portable LED projectors imported from Malaysia. Inspection revealed that the CCC marks on the products were counterfeit and did not correspond to any valid certification record in the CNCA database. The importer was fined RMB 60,000 and the goods were ordered to be re-exported.

title: Smart watches blocked at Beijing Customs for encryption declaration failure
summary: Beijing Customs flagged a shipment of smart watches imported from Singapore. The devices contained encrypted data storage and communication functions, which triggered commercial encryption import controls. The importer failed to declare the encryption capability and lacked the required import license. Goods were detained and a penalty of RMB 52,000 was applied.

title: Wireless keyboards without SRRC rejected at Guangzhou Customs
summary: Guangzhou Customs rejected a shipment of Bluetooth keyboards imported from Thailand. The importer had classified them as standard computer peripherals and was unaware that the Bluetooth radio transmitter required SRRC Type Approval. The goods were returned at the importer's expense and a fine of RMB 18,000 was issued.

title: VR headsets seized at Shanghai Port for missing SRRC approval
summary: Shanghai Customs detained a shipment of Wi-Fi-enabled VR headsets imported from South Korea. The importer had obtained CCC certification but failed to secure SRRC Type Approval for the built-in Wi-Fi and Bluetooth modules. The goods were held and a fine of RMB 55,000 was imposed before the importer completed the SRRC application.

title: Drone camera payloads blocked for dual-use export controls
summary: Shenzhen Customs intercepted a shipment of thermal imaging camera payloads intended for commercial drones. The exporter was unaware that specific camera modules with infrared capabilities fall under China's Dual-Use Items Export Control List. A fine of RMB 120,000 was imposed and the export license application was required before shipment could proceed.

title: E-scooters denied air freight for undeclared lithium batteries
summary: A batch of electric scooters destined for Europe was rejected for air freight at Guangzhou Baiyun Airport. The exporter failed to declare the large-capacity lithium batteries under UN3171 dangerous goods regulations. The shipment was re-routed to ocean freight, incurring additional logistics costs and a customs penalty of RMB 25,000.

title: 3D printers fined for missing CCC at export inspection
summary: Ningbo Customs flagged a consignment of FDM 3D printers declared for export. The products were classified as IT equipment and lacked valid CCC certification. The exporter argued the products were for overseas markets and should be exempt, but Customs ruled that CCC applies to all units manufactured in China regardless of destination. Fine of RMB 40,000 imposed.

title: Walkie-talkies returned for lack of SRRC and frequency approval
summary: Dalian Customs rejected a shipment of UHF walkie-talkies imported from Malaysia. The importer was unaware that two-way radios require both SRRC Type Approval and, for certain frequency bands, additional spectrum allocation approval from MIIT. The goods were returned and the importer fined RMB 32,000.

title: Children's smart watches seized for excessive data collection at import inspection
summary: Shanghai Customs detained a shipment of children's smart watches imported from Hong Kong. In addition to missing SRRC Type Approval for the cellular and Wi-Fi modules, the devices were found to have voice recording and GPS tracking features that required separate cybersecurity review. The importer was fined RMB 68,000 and required to submit a data privacy compliance assessment before release.

title: Fitness trackers exported without SRRC approval stopped at Yantian Port
summary: Yantian Customs, Shenzhen, blocked the export of 5,000 Bluetooth-enabled fitness trackers destined for Brazil. The exporter had obtained CCC certification but was unaware that SRRC Type Approval is required even for products manufactured solely for export. Goods were held for 3 weeks while the SRRC application was expedited. A fine of RMB 35,000 was imposed.

title: Nintendo Switch consoles blocked for missing CCC and game content review
summary: A batch of game consoles was flagged at Xiamen Customs during export inspection. The exporter had classified the consoles under a non-CCC HS Code, but Customs re-classified them as IT equipment requiring CCC certification. Additionally, pre-installed games raised content review compliance questions. The exporter was fined RMB 55,000.

title: Wireless gaming controllers fined for dual SRRC and encryption violation
summary: Guangzhou Customs inspected a shipment of premium wireless gaming controllers with programmable buttons and onboard memory. The Bluetooth module required SRRC approval, and the encrypted firmware storage triggered commercial encryption export controls. The exporter, unfamiliar with dual-use encryption rules, was fined RMB 42,000 and the shipment was delayed by 4 weeks.

title: Smart door locks with biometric sensors blocked at import
summary: Tianjin Customs inspected a consignment of smart door locks with fingerprint and facial recognition features imported from South Korea. Beyond SRRC for the Bluetooth/Wi-Fi modules, Customs flagged the biometric data collection capability as requiring a cybersecurity review. The importer was fined RMB 75,000 and required to obtain both SRRC and a cybersecurity assessment.

title: Zigbee smart sensors exported without SRRC caught at Shenzhen
summary: Shenzhen Customs intercepted a shipment of Zigbee-based temperature and humidity sensors destined for a European smart home brand. The exporter believed that low-power Zigbee devices were exempt from SRRC, but MIIT regulations require approval for all intentional radio transmitters regardless of power output. A fine of RMB 22,000 was imposed on 2,000 units.

title: E-cigarette vaping devices seized for undeclared lithium batteries and CCC absence
summary: Huangpu Customs, Guangzhou, seized a shipment of rechargeable vape pens and pod systems destined for the UK. The devices contained built-in lithium batteries that were not declared as dangerous goods, and the electronic components lacked CCC certification. The exporter was fined RMB 48,000 and the goods were held pending proper dangerous goods packaging and certification.

title: Disposable vapes imported without nicotine content declaration fined at Beijing
summary: Beijing Customs detained a commercial shipment of disposable electronic cigarettes imported from the UK. Beyond CCC certification for the electronic components, China's tobacco monopoly regulations require special import licensing and nicotine content declaration for all vaping products. The importer was unaware of the dual regulatory framework. Fine of RMB 90,000 and mandatory return of goods was ordered.

title: Self-balancing hoverboards returned at Long Beach after China export violation flagged
summary: A shipment of self-balancing hoverboards imported from China was flagged during U.S. entry at Long Beach. The U.S. Customs notified Chinese counterparts after discovering the lithium batteries were improperly declared. Investigation revealed the exporter had not completed UN38.3 testing and lacked the mandatory Safety Data Sheet and Label. The exporter was fined RMB 38,000 by Chinese Customs and the goods were returned.

title: High-power walkie-talkies exported without frequency approval intercepted
summary: Xiamen Customs intercepted a shipment of high-power DMR handheld radios destined for Southeast Asia. Although the exporter had SRRC Type Approval, the specific UHF frequencies used by the devices exceeded the approved range in the SRRC certificate, requiring additional frequency allocation approval from MIIT. The exporter was fined RMB 28,000 for the mismatch between approved and actual specifications.
